blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
Here are the best Android apps available on the Google Play Store for playing **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or **Forehead Poker**):

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a dedicated single-player, offline card game built entirely around the Blind Man's Bluff mechanic. You are dealt a card that you cannot see, but you can see your AI opponent's card. You must read the AI's facial expressions and betting patterns to guess the value of your own card. 
* **Key Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard) with distinct bluffing behaviors.
  * A strategic betting system featuring Call, Half, Die, and All-In options.
  * Customization shop to unlock different avatars, outfits, card decks, and table themes using in-game chips.
  * 100% offline play with no internet or account required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Description:** This app is designed to let you play the classic forehead-poker style game with friends and family. It digitizes the experience of looking only at your opponent's card to predict your victory.
* **Key Features:**
  * Local multiplayer focus, making it a great party tool to play with people in the same room.
  * Clean, user-friendly interface designed to track scores and card distributions easily.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** A lightweight utility and simulation app designed to facilitate a physical game of Indian Poker/Blind Man's Bluff. If you are hanging out with a group of friends and don't have a physical deck of cards, this app acts as the digital deck.
* **Key Features:**
  * Designed for multiple players in a group.
  * Simple setup that allows players to pass the phone or display cards outward so others can see them.

forehead poker app Android.response:
Depending on whether you are looking for the **traditional card game** (also known as *Indian Poker* or *Blind Man's Bluff*, where you hold a playing card to your forehead) or the **party guessing game** (where you hold the phone to your forehead to guess words), there are excellent Android apps available for both on the Google Play Store:

### 1. Traditional "Forehead Poker" (Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff)
In these games, you see your opponent's card but not your own, and you must bet based on their reactions and what you can deduce.

*   **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
    *   **Description:** This is a dedicated, offline single-player version of Blind Man's Bluff (Forehead Poker). You play head-to-head against an AI dealer. The game features three difficulty levels where the AI bluffs, reacts, and shows "tells" that you have to read to guess the strength of your hidden card.
*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc)
    *   **Description:** A mobile adaptation of the classic forehead card game designed to be played with friends and family. It focuses on the psychological aspect of the game, allowing you to read your opponents' expressions and manage your betting strategy.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
    *   **Description:** A straightforward, lightweight simulation of the classic card game. It is designed for local multiplayer setups where players can use the app to simulate the deck and betting rounds.

---

### 2. "Forehead" Party Games (Heads Up! & Charades)
If you are looking for the popular party game where you hold the phone to your forehead and your friends give you clues to guess a word, these are the top choices:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
    *   **Description:** The official, wildly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You place the phone on your forehead, and your friends shout clues, sing, or act out the word on the screen. It features dozens of themed decks (movies, animals, celebrities) and lets you record video of your friends' hilarious clues.
*   **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode)
    *   **Description:** A highly polished alternative to Heads Up. It features a "Team Mode" for competitive game nights, custom deck creation, and support for over 25 languages. You tilt the phone down when you guess correctly and up to pass.
*   **Hands Up: ForeHead Charades!**
    *   **Description:** A fast-paced party game with a wide variety of categories. It is particularly popular for its diverse decks, ranging from family-friendly trivia to adult-themed party decks.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for Android apps featuring card games where you cannot see your own cards, here are the best real and currently available options on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Description:** This is a single-player psychological card game based on the classic "Blind Man's Bluff" (also known as Indian Poker). In this game, your card is dealt face-down to you but face-up to your AI opponent—meaning you can see their card, but you can never see your own. You have to read the AI's facial expressions, tells, and betting patterns to guess the value of your card and decide whether to Call, Raise, Fold, or go All-In. 

### 2. **Boardible: Games for Groups**
* **Description:** Boardible is a companion and digital board game platform that includes a fully playable version of **Hanabi**. Hanabi is an award-winning cooperative card game where players work together to launch a grand fireworks display. The core mechanic is that you hold your hand of cards facing outward. You can see everyone else's cards, but you cannot see your own. You must rely on limited, strategic clues given by your teammates to figure out what is in your hand and play the correct cards in sequence.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Description:** If you are looking for a simple, local multiplayer tool to play the classic "card on the forehead" game with friends, this app is a direct digital simulation of Indian Poker. It allows multiple players to gather around, hide their own cards from themselves while displaying them to the rest of the group, and play the traditional bluffing game using their mobile devices.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
Depending on whether you are looking for a traditional playing card game or a party word-guessing game, there are two main types of "forehead card games" available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. Traditional Card Games (Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff)
If you are looking for the classic card game where players hold a standard playing card to their forehead and bet on who has the highest card without looking at their own:

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc): This app lets you play the classic psychological card game. You "hold" your card to your forehead, look at your opponent's card, read their facial expressions, and bet chips based on who you think has the higher card. 
*   **Blindex**: A single-player version of Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker). You hold your virtual card to your head, read the AI's tells, and bet across three different difficulty levels.

---

### 2. Party & Charades Games (Phone-on-Forehead Word Games)
If you are looking for the party game where your phone acts as a "card" on your forehead and you have to guess the word on the screen using clues from your friends:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises): The incredibly popular party game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You choose a category (like Celebrities, Movies, or Animals), hold the phone to your forehead, and try to guess the words as your friends shout clues, sing, or act them out. Tilting the phone down marks a correct guess, while tilting it up passes.
*   **Guess Up - Party Charades** (by Cosmicode): A highly-rated alternative to Heads Up! that features over 100 fun decks. It includes a team mode, the ability to record your friends' hilarious reactions while you guess, and an option to create your own custom card decks.
*   **Charades & Headbands Game** (also known as *Charades App - What am I?*): A great multiplayer guessing game where you place the device on your forehead and ask your friends to help you guess the word through mimes, dancing, or singing. It features over 60 diverse decks, including pop culture, gaming, and anime themes.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline, single-player bluffing card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Liar's Cards - Ship of Deceit** (by Prism113)
* **The Vibe:** Dark, high-stakes pirate deception.
* **How it plays:** Heavily inspired by the popular PC game *Liar's Bar*, this game is set on a cursed pirate ship. You are dealt a hand of cards and must play them face-down, claiming they match a specific rank (like Kings or Queens). If an opponent calls your bluff and catches you lying, you must participate in a "poisoned mug challenge" (a Russian-roulette-style game of chance). 
* **Offline Mode:** It features a fully functional offline single-player mode where you play against AI pirate opponents.

### 2. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **The Vibe:** Classic, fast-paced, and strategic.
* **How it plays:** This is a digital adaptation of the classic card game "Cheat" (also known as *I Doubt It* or *BS*). The goal is to empty your hand by discarding cards face-down and declaring their rank. You can lie about what you are discarding, but if a CPU opponent calls "Bluff!" and catches you, you have to pick up the entire pile. It also includes fun strategic items like "Bluff Shields" and "Lie Counters."
* **Offline Mode:** Designed for quick offline sessions, putting you in 4-player battles against 3 CPU opponents.

### 3. **Bluff Card Game Offline**
* **The Vibe:** Clean, traditional, and straightforward.
* **How it plays:** A highly polished version of the traditional *I Doubt It* / *Bluff* card game. It features realistic card animations and a smooth user interface.
* **Offline Mode:** Built specifically for offline play with no internet or Wi-Fi required. It offers a 2-Player Mode (1v1 against a smart AI) and a 4-Player Mode (against multiple CPU opponents) with adjustable difficulty.

### 4. **Cleverfox - I Doubt It**
* **The Vibe:** Customizable and modern.
* **How it plays:** Another excellent version of "Cheat." What makes *Cleverfox* stand out is the ability to customize the rules before the game starts (e.g., choosing whether players must discard the same rank, one rank higher, one rank lower, or random cards). 
* **Offline Mode:** You can play entirely offline against computer-controlled opponents.

### 5. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YOAMB B.V.)
* **The Vibe:** Classic pirate bluffing (dice-based).
* **How it plays:** While technically a *dice* game rather than a card game, it is the ultimate classic bluffing game (also known as *Perudo* or *Dudo*). Players hide their dice under cups, roll them, and take turns bidding on how many of a certain face value exist across the entire table. 
* **Offline Mode:** Despite "Online" being in the title, the app features a robust **Offline Practice Mode** with highly intelligent AI opponents, making it perfect for offline single-player sessions.

best offline single player card game And.response:
Here are the best offline, single-player card games available on the Google Play Store, ranging from modern roguelike deckbuilders to classic card game collections:

### **Top Roguelike Deckbuilders & RPGs**
*   **Balatro**
    *   **Description:** A massive hit poker-themed roguelike deckbuilder. Instead of fighting monsters, you play illegal poker hands to score points, discover game-changing Joker cards that trigger wild score multipliers, and buy tarot cards to alter your deck. It is incredibly addictive, highly strategic, and runs perfectly offline.
*   **Slay the Spire**
    *   **Description:** The gold standard of roguelike deckbuilders. You choose one of four unique characters, each with their own distinct card pool and playstyle, and fight your way up a procedurally generated spire. It is a premium game with endless replayability and zero internet connection required.
*   **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
    *   **Description:** A premium, offline-first RPG card game designed specifically for portrait mode. It features over 900 hand-illustrated cards, multiple classes (like Rogue, Warrior, and Seeker), and deep strategic choices. There are no microtransactions or ads, making it a complete, high-quality offline experience.
*   **Night of the Full Moon**
    *   **Description:** A dark-fantasy, story-driven roguelike deckbuilder based on the Little Red Riding Hood fairy tale. It offers beautiful artwork, multiple character classes, and highly tactical turn-based combat. The base game is free to play and works entirely offline.

### **Tactical & Puzzle Card Games**
*   **Card Thief**
    *   **Description:** A unique stealth-puzzle card game played on a 3x3 grid. You play as a thief moving through cards representing guards, torches, and obstacles. You must manage your stealth points, sneak past enemies, and steal treasure. It is perfect for quick, offline, single-player sessions.
*   **Cultist Simulator**
    *   **Description:** A narrative-driven, rogue-lite card game where you play as a seeker after unholy mysteries in a 1920s-themed setting. You combine cards on a board to perform actions, study lore, and recruit followers while trying to avoid going mad or getting caught by the authorities. It is deeply atmospheric and fully offline.

### **Classic & Traditional Card Games**
*   **Microsoft Solitaire Collection**
    *   **Description:** The ultimate collection of classic solitaire games. It includes five of the best solitaire variants: Klondike, Spider, FreeCell, Pyramid, and TriPeaks. All modes are fully playable offline, and your progress syncs up whenever you reconnect to the internet.
*   **Ultimate Offline Card Games**
    *   **Description:** If you prefer traditional card games, this is a fantastic all-in-one package. It lets you play popular games like Rummy 500, Spades, Hearts, Gin Rummy, and Canasta offline against smart AI opponents. It features clean graphics, smooth animations, and requires no internet connection.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The specific Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker**) is called **Blindex**.

**Blindex** is a free, offline, single-player card game where you see your opponent's card but never your own, exactly matching the rules of Blind Man's Bluff [1][2].

### Key Features of Blindex:
| Feature | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Game Type** | Single-player psychological card game against an AI dealer [1] |
| **Offline Play** | 100% offline; no internet or Wi-Fi required [1][2] |
| **Cost** | 100% free with **no ads** and **no in-app purchases** [1] |
| **Difficulty** | Three difficulty levels with different AI bluffing styles [1] |
| **Availability** | Available worldwide on **Google Play** [1] |

**Download Link:**  
You can find it on Google Play by searching for "**Blindex - Blind Card Game**" or visiting the app page directly: `com.blindex.app` [2].

*Note: Many other apps named "Bluff" on Android (e.g., by Oneiric Gaming or Floating Ninja Games) refer to a different game known as "Cheat" or "I Doubt It," where players try to get rid of all their cards by lying about their hand [6][7][8]. These are not Blind Man's Bluff.*

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no official Android app called **“Forehead Poker,”** but the game you’re likely referring to is **Indian Poker** (also known as “Forehead Poker”), where players hold a single card to their forehead and guess their rank based on others’ reactions. While no dedicated “Forehead Poker” app exists on Google Play, you can play this game using these real Android apps:

### Best Android Apps for Forehead/Indian Poker Style Games:

| App Name | Description | Google Play Link |
|----------|-------------|------------------|
| **Heads Up Poker** | Play Texas Hold’em with a friend on one device. Tap-hold cards to view your hand—simulating the “forehead” reveal style. | [Install on Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.bearian.heads_up_poker) [11][10] |
| **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** | Free Texas Hold’em game where you hold your phone to your forehead to see your cards—explicitly designed for this mechanic. | [Install on Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.comunix.pokerface) [1][2][8] |
| **Heads Up Charades!** | Party game where you hold the phone to your forehead to guess words from clues (Indian Poker style), but not poker-specific. | [Install on Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.global.poker.headsup.online) [6] |

### Key Recommendation:
**Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** is the closest match—it’s a free Texas Hold’em app by Comunix Ltd. that explicitly lets you **hold your phone to your forehead to see your cards**, mimicking the Forehead/Indian Poker experience [1][2]. It’s available on Android, updated May 22, 2025, and downloaded ~48K times monthly [8].

If you want a pure Indian Poker (single-card forehead) game, you may need to play it manually with friends using physical cards, as no dedicated app exists for that exact variant on Android yet [17].

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you are describing is most likely **Heads!**, a popular Android app where players hold their phone above their head showing a card they cannot see themselves, while others try to guess it.

However, if you are looking for a specific game mechanic where **your own hand is hidden from you but visible to everyone else** (often called "reverse hands" or "blind hands"), this is not a standard rule in a single famous commercial app, but rather a feature found in:

*   **Tabletop Simulator** (Android version or via Steam Link): This app allows you to enable a "Reverse" option for hands, making cards visible to others but hidden from the player holding them [3].
*   **VirtualTableTop.io**: A web-based virtual table that supports hidden cards and specific visibility rules for multiplayer games [10].
*   **Rummy (by LITE Games)**: While a standard Rummy app, it allows you to hide cards on the table, though it does not inherently hide your own view from yourself in the "reverse" mechanic [2].

If you meant the game where you **guess a card on your head** without seeing it, download **Heads!** on Google Play. It is the definitive app for this specific "can't see your own card" party game experience.

If you are looking for a game where the **entire hand is invisible to you** (a "blind" variant), you may need to use a **Card Game Simulator** or **Tabletop Simulator** and manually configure the "Reverse" hand setting, as no major standalone commercial Android game is built exclusively around this specific "blind self" mechanic as its primary rule [4][5].

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android card game you’re describing is most likely **Heads Up!**, a popular party game where one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward, and friends give clues to help them guess the word or phrase shown [1][2][4].

### Key Features of **Heads Up!**:
- **How to play**: Choose a category (e.g., animals, celebrities), hold the phone to your forehead, and guess the word based on friends’ clues before the 60-second timer runs out [1][10].
- **Social gameplay**: Designed for groups—friends act out, describe, or impersonate the word while the guesser can’t see the screen [2][9].
- **Popularity**: Gained fame through Ellen DeGeneres, who plays it on her show [4].
- **Available on Google Play**: The official app is by Warner Bros. International Enterprises (ID: `com.wb.headsup`) [1].

### Similar Alternatives:
If you’re looking for variations, these Android apps also support the “forehead game” style:
- **Charades & Headbands Game** (`net.artgamestudio.charadesapp`) – Offers 60+ charades decks and custom options [3].
- **For Your Head** – A trivia and guessing game where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess while others give clues [5].
- **HeadsUp-Headbands** – Includes brain teaser questions and word-guessing mechanics [8].

Note: There’s also a traditional card game called **Indian Poker** (or Blind Man’s Bluff) where players hold a physical card to their forehead, but that’s not an Android app—it uses a real deck of cards [7][13][15].

For the mobile version, **Heads Up!** is the most recognized and widely used app matching your description.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
The best match for a **single-player bluffing card game** on **Android** that works **offline** is **Blindex** (Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker) [1].

### Why Blindex fits your criteria:
| Feature | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| **Game Type** | **Blind Man's Bluff** (also called Indian Poker or Forehead Poker), a classic **bluffing** game where you read AI tells [1] |
| **Single-Player** | Play head-to-head against an **AI dealer** with 3 difficulty levels [1] |
| **Offline** | Runs **100% offline** — no internet required [1] |
| **Cost** | **Free**, no ads, no in-app purchases [1] |
| **Availability** | Available on **Google Play Store** for Android [1] |

### Alternative options (less focused on bluffing):
- **Bluff Offline** (also called *Cheat* or *I Doubt It*) — a bluffing game where you lie about card moves and AI can call your bluff [6]. However, specific Google Play Store availability details are less clear than Blindex.
- **CardGames.io** — includes offline single-player modes with AI, but bluffing isn't its primary focus [2].

**Recommendation:** Download **Blindex** directly from Google Play for the most authentic single-player bluffing experience offline [1].

| **Spider Solitaire** | Challenging variant of solitaire, great for single-player focus [2] |
| **Gwent: The Witcher Card Game** | Story-driven, offline mode available, deep strategy (though primarily online) [3] |
| **Card Crawl** | Unique dungeon-crawling card game, fully offline, single-player only [4] |
| **Slay the Spire** | Premium deck-building RPG, offline, highly acclaimed for single-player depth [5] |

For a pure, traditional card experience, **Solitaire by MobilityWare** is the safest and most enjoyable choice. If you prefer something more strategic or narrative-driven, **Slay the Spire** or **Card Crawl** are excellent premium alternatives.

*Note: Search results were not provided, so recommendations are based on well-established, highly-rated Android card games known for offline single-player functionality.*

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
**Blindex** is a free, offline single-player Android app that simulates Blind Man's Bluff (also known as Indian Poker or Forehead Poker). You play head-to-head against AI opponents with three difficulty levels featuring different tells and bluffing styles. Earn points to unlock cosmetics like hairstyles, outfits, card decks, and tables. No ads or in-app purchases; fully offline. Available on the Google Play Store.[[1]](https://blindex.app/)

Other "Bluff" apps on the Play Store (such as Bluff Online or Bluff Card Game) are unrelated shedding/bluffing games and not the poker variant.

forehead poker app Android.response:
**Blindex — Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker)**  
Free offline single-player Android app for playing Forehead Poker (Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker) against AI opponents with different difficulty levels and bluffing styles. Available on Google Play.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
**No dedicated full digital Hanabi (or similar "can't see your own cards") card game app appears available on the Google Play Store.**

The closest real apps are utilities for the physical **Hanabi** board game:

- **Hanabi Tracker** – Free utility app that tracks clues and card info during a physical Hanabi game so you can focus on strategy. 
- **Hanabi-Navi** – Companion/reference app for Hanabi gameplay details. 

You can play the official Hanabi digitally via browser on Android at Board Game Arena (boardgamearena.com).

Android card game where you hold card to.response: **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.) – The top Android app matching this description. Players hold their phone to their forehead (like a card or headband) and guess words/phrases from categories while friends give clues before the timer runs out. Available on Google Play Store.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
**Bluff Card Game** (by oengines or similar variants like "Bluff Card Game Offline"): Classic single-player offline bluffing game (also known as Cheat, BS, or I Doubt It). Lie about the cards you play and call out opponents' bluffs against AI.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.oengines.bluffoffline)

**Liar's Dice**: Bluffing dice/card hybrid with offline AI opponents for practice. Call bluffs on dice rolls in this classic game.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.yoambulante.liarsdicepro)

**Truco**: Strategic offline card game with heavy bluffing elements (popular in Brazil). Play single-player matches against AI with no internet needed.[[3]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=truco.card.game.brasil.trick.strategic.poker.online.multiplayer.offline.game)

These are all available on the Google Play Store and support offline single-player modes against AI.
